# AUTO-GENERATED by split_acceptance_tests.py
# Each original test function was split into one function per top-level
# assertion so pytest reports per-check pass/fail counts.
import re

def test_visualization_barplot_example_suppresses_output_with_semicolon__a1():
    source = open('/workspace/doc/source/visualization.rst', encoding='utf-8').read()
    pattern = re.compile("\n        ^\\s*@savefig\\ bar_plot_ex\\.png\\s*$\n        \\n\n        ^\\s*df\\.iloc\\[5\\]\\.plot\\.bar\\(\\)\\s*;?\\s*$\n        \\n\n        ^\\s*plt\\.axhline\\(0,\\s*color='k'\\)\\s*;?\\s*$\n        ", re.MULTILINE | re.VERBOSE)
    m = pattern.search(source)
    assert m is not None, "Expected to find the bar plot example block with @savefig bar_plot_ex.png, followed by df.iloc[5].plot.bar() and plt.axhline(0, color='k') on the next lines."

def test_visualization_barplot_example_suppresses_output_with_semicolon__a2():
    source = open('/workspace/doc/source/visualization.rst', encoding='utf-8').read()
    pattern = re.compile("\n        ^\\s*@savefig\\ bar_plot_ex\\.png\\s*$\n        \\n\n        ^\\s*df\\.iloc\\[5\\]\\.plot\\.bar\\(\\)\\s*;?\\s*$\n        \\n\n        ^\\s*plt\\.axhline\\(0,\\s*color='k'\\)\\s*;?\\s*$\n        ", re.MULTILINE | re.VERBOSE)
    m = pattern.search(source)
    df_line = m.group(0).splitlines()[-2].strip()
    axhline_line = m.group(0).splitlines()[-1].strip()
    assert not df_line.endswith(';'), "The df.iloc[5].plot.bar() line should not end with ';' in this example; the review change puts the semicolon on the second line to suppress output."

def test_visualization_barplot_example_suppresses_output_with_semicolon__a3():
    source = open('/workspace/doc/source/visualization.rst', encoding='utf-8').read()
    pattern = re.compile("\n        ^\\s*@savefig\\ bar_plot_ex\\.png\\s*$\n        \\n\n        ^\\s*df\\.iloc\\[5\\]\\.plot\\.bar\\(\\)\\s*;?\\s*$\n        \\n\n        ^\\s*plt\\.axhline\\(0,\\s*color='k'\\)\\s*;?\\s*$\n        ", re.MULTILINE | re.VERBOSE)
    m = pattern.search(source)
    df_line = m.group(0).splitlines()[-2].strip()
    axhline_line = m.group(0).splitlines()[-1].strip()
    assert axhline_line.endswith(';'), "Expected the plt.axhline(0, color='k') line to end with ';' to suppress the plot representation in rendered HTML output."
